text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Geopolitical tensions (Middle East, China export controls) are a major overhang, but lower Treasury yields offer some relief for growth stocks. Nvidia's investment in AI cloud is a bright spot for chips, but China's potential retaliation is a big concern.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.24%) and SPAL (-51.33%) are in deep trouble, both leveraged and with blown stops. QQQ (-2.6%), SMH (-12.19%), and QQQI (-3.79%) are also flagged "SELL". Your margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on any developments regarding China's potential AI tech export controls – this could hit your core holdings hard. Also, monitor geopolitical stability in the Middle East.

Bottom line: Aggressively manage risk on your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially given the macro headwinds.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 is far above the current price of 136.81. This leveraged ETF is down 46.24%; consider exiting to protect capital.
SPAL: This new, leveraged position is down 51.33% with its stop at 21.38 well above the current 12.24. Consider exiting to prevent further decay.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Geopolitical risks, especially China's potential tech export controls, create too much uncertainty for new long positions.
